condensed_reasoning:
## Research

I approached this fast-moving geopolitical question by first parsing the resolution criteria, then researching the current state via my `research` tool, which spun up a nested research agent. That agent cross-validated Wikipedia (Islamabad Memorandum, 2026 Iran war, 2026 Iran war ceasefire), Al Jazeera, CBS, CNN, and Good Morning America timelines, plus prediction markets from Manifold and Polymarket. Several sub-agent tool calls failed (asknews 403 rate/tier limits, a CNN 451 block, one sibling-tool error), but I recovered by cross-validating every fact from multiple independent sources.

## Key Evidence

- **No ceasefire in effect as of July 13, 2026.** The June 17 Islamabad MoU has collapsed; Trump declared it **"over" on July 8** after Iran struck ships in the Strait of Hormuz and the US struck Iranian territory.
- **Active escalation, not de-escalation:** the US conducted a **second consecutive night of strikes July 12–13** (dozens of targets, expanding beyond coastal areas); Iran's IRGC declared the **Strait of Hormuz "closed until further notice"** and struck US bases across Bahrain, Kuwait, Qatar, Jordan, and Oman; Jordan intercepted four Iranian missiles July 13.
- **Trump rhetoric hostile:** called Iran "sick people"/"scum," threatened "1000 Missiles are Locked and Loaded"; claimed July 10 Iran "asked us to continue talks" — which **Iran's foreign ministry denied**.
- **Countervailing path:** this dyad has repeatedly returned to ceasefires (April 8 → extended April 21 → MoU June 17), with heavy mediator engagement (Pakistan/Qatar/Oman) and Trump's stated aversion to long-term military action ($113B war cost pressure).
- **Market anchor:** Manifold "any second 2026 ceasefire" at **~58%** (July 13), but this covers a ceasefire occurring anytime through Dec 2026 — far looser than the question's specific-date + mutual-observance + 3-clean-days condition.

## Conclusion

I parsed YES as requiring ALL of: (1) a mutually observed ceasefire in effect on July 23; (2) no direct US↔Iran strikes during July 21–23 UTC; (3) the "over" declaration reversed. Against the current escalatory trajectory, YES requires a new ceasefire re-brokered within ~8 days AND holding cleanly for the final 3 days. I decomposed: **P(ceasefire in effect ~July 21) ≈ 0.35–0.40 × P(3 clean days | fragile fresh ceasefire) ≈ 0.6–0.7 ≈ 0.24**. My reflection tool produced a factor sum implying ~0.23 and a tentative 0.26; the premortem (counterargument landing at 0.40) was reviewed and **approved**. I concluded a final estimate of **~0.25** (logit −1.05), well below the loose Manifold anchor because the specific-date, mutual-observance, and 3-clean-day conjunction is genuinely narrower.

capability_gaps: No access to the verbatim question resolution text in the parent trace — the agent had to infer the exact YES conditions, which is the largest source of potential miscalibration.
subtle_bugs: asknews n_articles>10 silently reserved for higher tiers and a separate billable-overage cap — both returned 403s that consumed turns without data.

Nested sub-agent tool failures are not reflected in parent per-tool metrics, which is why the agent's self-reported 'no tool failures' looked consistent with session-level ground truth while being factually wrong.

classification.approach: Qualitative decomposition — P(ceasefire re-brokered by ~July 21) × P(holds clean during July 21-23 window) — anchored against prediction markets and the conflict's ceasefire-reversion history.
classification.key_uncertainty: Whether mediators can broker a new ceasefire within ~8 days during active escalation AND whether any such ceasefire survives the exact July 21-23 window without a single direct strike.

risk_flags.evidence: The agent never saw the verbatim resolution text in the parent trace — it inferred the YES conditions (mutual observance on July 23, no strikes July 21-23 UTC, reversal of 'over' declaration). If the actual resolution is looser (e.g. any announced pause counts, or no explicit 3-clean-day requirement), the strict conjunction penalty of ~0.25 would be too low. The premortem noted resolution criteria were 'provided in full' but the specific 3-clean-day window appears agent-constructed.

The agent decomposed P(ceasefire in effect ~July 21) at 0.35-0.40 during a night when the US was conducting a SECOND consecutive night of expanding strikes, IRGC closed Hormuz, and Trump threatened '1000 missiles.' Given active upward escalation just 8-10 days from resolution, a ceasefire being re-brokered AND holding 3 clean days may be lower than 0.25; a case exists for ~0.15-0.18. This is minor given genuine reversion history.

strengths: Correctly recognized that the Manifold ~58% 'any second 2026 ceasefire' market covers a far looser condition (anytime through Dec 2026) than the specific-date + 3-clean-day requirement, and explicitly adjusted well below the anchor rather than deferring to it.
Used a clean two-part decomposition — P(ceasefire re-brokered by ~July 21) × P(holds cleanly July 21-23) — which is the natural structure for a conflict-status question on a tight timeline.
Cross-validated every fact from multiple independent sources (Wikipedia, Al Jazeera, CBS, GMA) after several sub-agent tool calls failed, so no conclusion rested on a single unverified source.

Genuinely weighed the countervailing reversion pattern (April 8 → extended → June 17 MoU) and Trump's win-claiming incentive via a substantive premortem landing at 0.40, rather than a token counterargument.
weaknesses: Did not surface the verbatim resolution criteria in the parent trace; the specific '3 clean days July 21-23 UTC' and 'reversal of over declaration' conditions were inferred, creating risk that the strictness penalty is miscalibrated to the actual resolution.

Anchored the P(ceasefire ~July 21) leg at 0.35-0.40 without checking whether the MoU's 60-day negotiating window (ending ~Aug 21) or any scheduled deadline falls near July 21-23 — a timing detail the agent itself flagged as decision-relevant in the premortem but never resolved.
The tool_audit claim of 'no tool failures' was inaccurate — the nested research agent hit asknews 403s, a CNN 451, and a sibling-tool error; these were immaterial but the self-report was wrong.

issues: asknews search_news failed twice (403: n_articles>10 tier restriction, then billable overage limit exhausted) — wasted two calls.
CNN WebFetch returned 451 (legal block); one web_search sibling call errored and required a retry.
Sub-agent tool failures are invisible to parent per-tool metrics, causing the agent's tool_audit self-report ('no tool failures') to be inaccurate.

evidence: All accessed sources are dated on or before July 13, 2026 (the forecast date); the question resolves July 23-24, 2026.
Prediction market recent_history stops at 2026-07-13; no post-cutoff data appears.
The forecast is properly forward-looking about the July 21-23 window with no knowledge of the outcome; premortem confirmed no pre-publication event issue.

actionable_improvements: Surface the verbatim resolution criteria in the parent trace before decomposing — inferring the '3 clean days' and 'reversal' conditions risks miscalibrating the strictness penalty.
When a timeline anchor exists (MoU 60-day window ending ~Aug 21), explicitly check whether it falls inside the resolution window; the agent flagged this as decision-relevant but left it unresolved.
Fix the tool_audit self-report to reflect sub-agent failures, or aggregate nested metrics into the parent so 'no tool failures' claims are accurate.
Set asknews n_articles<=10 by default to avoid the tier 403, and detect overage exhaustion early to skip wasted calls.
